calendar.matters:
PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
Off Calendar Jan-14-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew Webb for Thickra Badi (Petitioner).

Mr. Webb stated that he has no more information about the missing passport, and that when his Client sent in the application for renewal of the passport, they returned the old one with the wholes punched. The Court stated that it now does appear that Ms. Badi was born at the time and date indicated while the Mother was on her way to the hospital. The Court granted the Petition to Establish Fact of Birth (subject to receipt and review of the order). Mr. Webb is to provide an order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
Off Calendar Nov-21-2018 Continued To Dec-19-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
Off Calendar Nov-07-2018 Continued To Nov-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF BIRTH
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Continued To Nov-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thickra Bodi appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: His name is Hany Farag. His Certification Number is 300129. He has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Arabic to English and English to Arabic.

The Court questions how Ms. Bodi got a passport and social security without a birth certificate and whether she signed the petitions. Ms. Bodi stated that she does not have a social security, that her Father produced a passport for her, that she does not know how, that she signed the petition and that she normally signs in capital block letters. The Court informed Ms. Bodi that there are still some problems with the petition which were set out in the tentative ruling, that boxes are incorrectly checked and noted that the petition was filed by a Friend, not Ms. Bodi. Ms. Bodi stated that she lives in New York and that this is the only person that can act for her. The Court stated that this is a problem, that Mr. Mohammed can not act for her, that he is not an Attorney, asks if he put her name on the petition, and stated that the petition needs to be filed by her, signed by her and correctly filled out by her. The Court also noted that the passport page (1 page) refers to page 27, but it does not have page 27 which might be helpful and explain how the passport was substantiated, that the letter from the hospital list the Mother's name as Gazowsky which is different for what is in the petition and that there is no explanation for the difference in the names. The Petition to Establish Fact of Birth is continued to November 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Bodi to file an amended petition to address all of the issues that were pointed out so that the Court can be in a position to act on the petition. The Court also stated that if possible, providing information on how they obtained the passport may be helpful. Ms. Bodi stated that she has no papers or information, and that her parents passed away eighteen years ago.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
